response:
A


The question focuses on ensuring stability (floating without tipping) for a repeated design of paper sailboats. This is best addressed via the engineering design process: build and iteratively test a prototype in water to directly observe and refine balance, center of gravity, and buoyancy before scaling to 20 boats. Option B only addresses aggregate weight (irrelevant to per-boat stability). Option C measures load capacity but does not test dynamic tipping in motion. Option D verifies material integrity but has no bearing on flotation dynamics. Thus, A is the only option that empirically validates the exact success criteria.
